tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'd like to discuss how we can set up our future conversations. I prefer receiving information in a detailed and precise manner—I'd appreciate thorough explanations with specific examples when possible. Could we establish a way to record these preferences so you can reference them in our future interactions?
I'd be happy to help establish that preference for our future conversations. Let me store that in memory so I can reference it in our upcoming interactions.

function_call.arguments: {"text": "User preferences for conversation style: prefers receiving information in a detailed and precise manner with thorough explanations and specific examples when possible."}

messages.content: Let me try that again with the correct function name:

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User preferences for conversation style: prefers receiving information in a detailed and precise manner with thorough explanations and specific examples when possible."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User preferences for conversation style: prefers receiving information in a detailed and precise manner with thorough explanations and specific examples when possible."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Your preference has been stored successfully. Going forward, I'll keep in mind that you prefer detailed, precise information with thorough explanations and specific examples whenever possible.

A few other preferences or settings I could store for future reference if you'd like:

- Your goals or projects I should be aware of
- Any specific topics or domains you work in
- Communication style tweaks (e.g., more formal/casual, bullet points vs. prose, etc.)
- Any topics you'd prefer to avoid

Just let me know if there's anything else you'd like me to remember, or if you ever want to review or update these preferences.

Now that we've established my communication preferences, I'd like to know if you have any existing memories or context about my work in molecular structure research techniques. What do you currently have stored regarding my research interests?

I don't have any existing memories stored regarding your work in molecular structure research techniques. The search returned no results, which means this is the first time you've mentioned this topic in our conversation.

If you'd like, you could share more details about your research area—such as the specific techniques you work with (e.g., X-ray crystallography, NMR spectroscopy, cryo-EM, computational modeling, etc.)—and I can store that information for reference in future conversations. This would help me tailor my responses to be more relevant and useful for your work.

I'd like to review what memories you currently have stored overall. Earlier you mentioned storing my communication preferences—could you show me all the memories you've retained so far? That would help me understand what context you're maintaining about our interactions.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User preferences for conversation style: prefers receiving information in a detailed and precise manner with thorough explanations and specific examples when possible."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Currently, I have just one memory stored — the communication preference we established earlier:

**Memory ID: m2**
- **Content:** "User preferences for conversation style: prefers receiving information in a detailed and precise manner with thorough explanations with specific examples when possible."

That's the only context being maintained at the moment. As we continue our conversations, I can store additional memories about your research, projects, or other preferences — just let me know what you'd like me to remember.

Now that I've reviewed what memories are stored, I can see we only have the one about my communication preferences. Since that's still relevant and useful, I don't think we need to remove anything at this point. However, if there were any older or irrelevant memories in the system, I'd want to clean those up to keep things organized. Are there any other memories beyond what you just showed me, or is this the complete picture?

Based on what I can see, this is the complete picture — we have only the one memory (ID: m2) about your communication preferences. There are no other memories stored, so there's nothing older or irrelevant to clean up at this point.

If you'd like to add more context about your molecular structure research or any other details in the future, just let me know and I can store those memories for you. And if you ever want to review what's stored or make changes, just ask!

I prefer receiving information in a detailed and precise manner—I'd appreciate thorough explanations with specific examples when possible. Could we establish a way to record these preferences so you can reference them in our future interactions?

A few other preferences or settings I could store for future reference if you'd like:

- Your goals or projects I should be aware of
- Any specific topics or domains you work in
- Communication style tweaks (e.g., more formal/casual, bullet points vs. prose, etc.)
- Any topics you'd prefer to avoid

Just let me know if there's anything else you'd like me to remember, or if you ever want to review or update these preferences.
